message.content:
## 코드 품질 리뷰

### 검토 대상
- D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\actions.ts
- D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainSearchForm.tsx
- D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainForm.tsx
- D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainDetailPanel.tsx
- D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainTable.tsx
- D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\app\(admin)\domain\page.tsx

### 프로젝트 컨텍스트
- Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript strict
- Server Actions: 'use server' + revalidatePath 패턴
- Client Components: react-hook-form, useRouter, shadcn Dialog

### 알려진 이슈 (이미 확인됨)
- DomainTable.tsx: `total`, `page`, `size` props가 선언되었으나 미사용 (DataTable이 자체 페이지네이션 처리하므로 props 제거 또는 사용 필요)

### 검토 항목
1. TypeScript 타입 안전성 (any 금지, 제네릭 올바른 사용)
2. React 패턴 (useEffect 올바른 deps, 불필요한 state, 메모이제이션 필요 여부)
3. Next.js 패턴 (useRouter 올바른 사용, Server Action 호출 패턴)
4. 보안 이슈 (XSS, 하드코딩된 값 등)
5. 미사용 imports, props, 변수
6. 에러 처리 누락

결론: "✅ QUALITY APPROVED" 또는 "❌ QUALITY ISSUES: [목록]"
중요도: CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR

content.text: I'll read all the files in parallel to gather the full context before reviewing.

content.content:
1	import { z } from 'zod'
2	
3	export const domainSearchSchema = z.object({
4	  keyword: z.string().optional(),
5	  page: z.coerce.number().default(0),
6	  size: z.coerce.number().default(20),
7	})
8	
9	export const domainSaveSchema = z.object({
10	  code: z.string().min(1).max(20),
11	  name: z.string().min(1).max(100),
12	  description: z.string().optional(),
13	})
14	
15	export type DomainSearchInput = z.infer<typeof domainSearchSchema>
16	export type DomainSaveInput = z.infer<typeof domainSaveSchema>
17

1	import 'server-only'
2	import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'
3	import type { DomainSearchInput } from './schema'
4	
5	export async function getDomains(params: DomainSearchInput) {
6	  const where = params.keyword
7	    ? {
8	        OR: [
9	          { code: { contains: params.keyword } },
10	          { name: { contains: params.keyword } },
11	        ],
12	      }
13	    : {}
14	
15	  const [items, total] = await prisma.$transaction([
16	    prisma.domain.findMany({
17	      where,
18	      select: { id: true, code: true, name: true, description: true, createdAt: true },
19	      skip: params.page * params.size,
20	      take: params.size,
21	      orderBy: { code: 'asc' },
22	    }),
23	    prisma.domain.count({ where }),
24	  ])
25	
26	  return { items, total }
27	}
28	
29	export async function getDomainById(id: number) {
30	  return prisma.domain.findUnique({ where: { id } })
31	}
32

1	export const ErrorCode = {
2	  UNAUTHORIZED: 'UNAUTHORIZED',
3	  FORBIDDEN: 'FORBIDDEN',
4	  NOT_FOUND: 'NOT_FOUND',
5	  VALIDATION_ERROR: 'VALIDATION_ERROR',
6	  INTERNAL_ERROR: 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
7	} as const
8	
9	export type ActionResult<T = void> =
10	  | { success: true; data: T }
11	  | { success: false; error: string; code: keyof typeof ErrorCode }
12

1	import { createSupabaseServerClient } from './supabase/server'
2	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
3	
4	export async function getSession() {
5	  const supabase = createSupabaseServerClient()
6	  const { data: { session } } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
7	  return session
8	}
9	
10	export async function requireAuth() {
11	  const session = await getSession()
12	  if (!session) redirect('/login')
13	  return session
14	}
15	
16	export async function requireAdmin() {
17	  const session = await requireAuth()
18	  if (session.user.app_metadata?.role !== 'ADMIN') redirect('/forbidden')
19	  return session
20	}
21

content.text:
I have all the context needed. Here is the complete review.

---

## Code Review: `features/domain`

---

### [IMPORTANT] DomainTable.tsx — Dead props in interface, partial pass-through

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainTable.tsx` lines 12–17 and 32

The interface declares `total`, `page`, and `size` as required props, but the component signature only destructures `items`. The three props are silently discarded. The call-site in `page.tsx` line 19 passes all four, so no compile error surfaces — but the interface is a contract that lies about what the component uses.

```tsx
// Current — props declared but never used inside the component
interface DomainTableProps {
    items: DomainItem[]
    total: number   // never read
    page: number    // never read
    size: number    // never read
}
export function DomainTable({ items }: DomainTableProps) { ... }

// Fix option A — remove unused props from the interface (if DataTable owns pagination)
interface DomainTableProps {
    items: DomainItem[]
}

// Fix option B — actually wire them into DataTable if it accepts external pagination
export function DomainTable({ items, total, page, size }: DomainTableProps) {
    // pass to DataTable when AG Grid is in server-side row model mode
}
```

This is the issue already flagged as "알려진 이슈." The right fix depends on whether AG Grid pagination is client-side (remove the props) or server-side (wire them). Given DataTable receives only `rowData` and `columnDefs`, the pagination is client-side, so **Option A is correct**: remove `total`, `page`, `size` from the interface.

---

### [IMPORTANT] types.ts — Duplicate type definitions with schema.ts

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\types.ts` line 6 and `D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\schema.ts` line 16

`DomainSaveInput` is defined twice — once manually in `types.ts` and once inferred from Zod in `schema.ts`. Both exist under the same name in different files.

```ts
// types.ts line 6 — manual definition
export type DomainSaveInput = { code: string; name: string; description?: string }

// schema.ts line 16 — inferred from Zod
export type DomainSaveInput = z.infer<typeof domainSaveSchema>
```

`DomainForm.tsx` imports from `schema.ts` (correct). Nothing currently imports `DomainSaveInput` from `types.ts`, but the duplicate is a maintenance hazard: a future developer importing from `types.ts` will get a type that may drift from the Zod schema. The `types.ts` definition should be removed and all consumers should import from `schema.ts`.

Similarly, `DomainSearchInput` is defined in both files (line 5 in `types.ts`, line 15 in `schema.ts`). `queries.ts` correctly imports from `schema.ts`. The redundant definitions in `types.ts` should be deleted.

---

### [IMPORTANT] actions.ts — No try/catch around Prisma calls; DB errors surface as unhandled exceptions

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\actions.ts` lines 13, 23, 30

All three Server Actions call Prisma without error handling. A unique-constraint violation on `domain.code`, a connection failure, or a foreign-key violation on `deleteDomain` (if other entities reference a domain) will throw an unhandled exception that Next.js will propagate as a 500 error page rather than a structured `ActionResult`.

The `ActionResult` type exists precisely to carry errors gracefully back to the client. The actions should catch and return a structured error:

```ts
// updateDomain example
export async function updateDomain(id: number, input: unknown): Promise<ActionResult> {
  await requireAdmin()
  const parsed = domainSaveSchema.safeParse(input)
  if (!parsed.success) return { success: false, error: '입력값이 올바르지 않습니다', code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR' }

  try {
    await prisma.domain.update({ where: { id }, data: parsed.data })
  } catch (e) {
    // Check for Prisma unique constraint (P2002) or record-not-found (P2025)
    return { success: false, error: '저장 중 오류가 발생했습니다', code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR' }
  }

  revalidatePath('/domain')
  return { success: true, data: undefined }
}
```

`deleteDomain` is the highest-risk case: if the domain is referenced by other records and no cascade is configured, the delete will throw a foreign-key constraint error that currently goes unhandled.

---

### [IMPORTANT] DomainDetailPanel.tsx — `deleting` state not reset on early-return error path

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainDetailPanel.tsx` lines 33–45

If `result.success` is false, `setDeleting(false)` has already been called on line 35 before the check — this is actually correct. No bug here on the happy/error path. However, there is a subtle race: if `deleteDomain` throws (which it can, per the issue above), the `catch` is unhandled at the component level. The `setDeleting(false)` on line 35 would not be reached, leaving the button permanently disabled. This is a direct consequence of the missing try/catch in actions.ts, but the component would also benefit from a defensive wrapper:

```tsx
try {
  const result = await deleteDomain(domain.id)
  if (!result.success) { toast.error(result.error); return }
  toast.success('도메인이 삭제되었습니다')
  onOpenChange(false)
  router.refresh()
} catch {
  toast.error('삭제 중 오류가 발생했습니다')
} finally {
  setDeleting(false)
}
```

---

### [MINOR] DomainForm.tsx — `router` imported and used, but `router.refresh()` + `onSuccess()` both called after save

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainForm.tsx` lines 36, 66–67

`router.refresh()` triggers a server re-fetch of the current page. `onSuccess()` is also called immediately after. In `DomainTable`, `onSuccess` is `() => {}` (no-op for create), and in `DomainDetailPanel`, `onSuccess` closes the detail panel — both fine. The sequencing is correct but worth noting: `router.refresh()` is async under the hood and the panel closes before data is guaranteed to be refreshed. This can cause a brief flash of stale data. If this is acceptable UX, no change needed; if not, `await router.refresh()` is not supported — the standard pattern is to rely solely on `revalidatePath` in the Server Action and call `router.refresh()` as a best-effort trigger.

No code change required, flagged for awareness only.

---

### [MINOR] DomainDetailPanel.tsx — `confirm()` for destructive action

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\components\DomainDetailPanel.tsx` line 31

```tsx
if (!confirm(`"${domain.name}" 도메인을 삭제하시겠습니까?`)) return
```

`window.confirm` is a browser-native blocking dialog with no styling control and poor accessibility. The project already uses shadcn/ui Dialogs throughout; a shadcn `AlertDialog` with "취소"/"삭제" buttons would be consistent with the rest of the UI. Minor UX/consistency issue, not a correctness bug.

---

### [MINOR] queries.ts — `getDomainById` fetches all columns; no `select` constraint

`D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\features\domain\queries.ts` line 30

```ts
return prisma.domain.findUnique({ where: { id } })
```

The CLAUDE.md review checklist explicitly requires "Prisma 쿼리에 적절한 `select`/`include` 제한 있음." `getDomainById` returns the full entity including any future sensitive columns added to the model. Add a `select` matching `DomainItem` fields for consistency with `getDomains`.

---

### Items confirmed PASS

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| `'use server'` on actions.ts | Pass |
| `'use client'` on all component files | Pass |
| `import 'server-only'` on queries.ts | Pass |
